description_long: This object consists of a cluster of stylized mushrooms with pink, dome-shaped caps and pale beige stems. The caps have a jagged, irregular lower edge and a subtle, painted texture that gives them a slightly rough, matte appearance. The largest mushroom stands centrally, surrounded by several smaller mushrooms branching off from the main stem at various heights and angles. The stems are cylindrical and smooth, with a light tan color that contrasts with the vibrant pink caps. The underside of the caps is darker, suggesting shadow or possible gill structures, though details are minimal. The overall form is organic and whimsical, making it suitable for decorative use in parks, gardens, or themed urban spaces.
description: A cluster of pink-capped mushrooms with jagged edges and pale stems, arranged in a whimsical, organic formation. The largest mushroom is central, surrounded by smaller ones branching off the main stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cluster, with the largest pink cap at the top and smaller mushrooms branching out visibly.
description_view_1: Top-down angled view highlighting the arrangement of the caps and the branching structure of the stems.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the backs of the caps and the main stem, with fewer mushrooms visible.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the staggered heights of the mushrooms and the curvature of the stems.
